Committee.CommitteeName: Subcommittee on Appropriations - Health, Mental Health, and Social Services
Special Interim Committee on Property Tax Reform
Special Committee on Property Tax Reform

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 200-B, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
State Representative Darin Chappell, a Republican, represents part of Greene County (District 137) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2022.
Prior to his time in the legislature, Chappell served as the city administrator for Chillicothe, Bolivar, and Seymour. He also is a former Missouri State University adjunct professor on the topics of the constitution and the impact of the foundational documents of American government, and public budgeting and finance at the graduate level. Chappell is a veteran of the United States Navy and the Army Reserves.
Passionate about serving his community, Chappell is involved with the Rotary Club, the MO Main Street Advisory Board, the American Legion, and the Veterans in Defense of Liberty organization. He is also an active member of the church of Christ in Fordland.
A graduate of Springfield Kickapoo High School, Chappell obtained a bachelor’s degree in Political Science and a master’s degree in Public Administration from Missouri State University.
Chappell and his wife reside in Rogersville, where his family has lived since 1836. They have three children and six grandchildren.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies financial requirements of the radioactive waste investigation fund
Prohibits state contracts for the purchase of electric vehicles unless the manufacturer certifies that the vehicles were produced without forced or child labor
Modifies provisions relating to the assessed valuation of residential real property
Establishes the "Fair Tax Act of 2025" which replaces the state individual and corporate income tax and the estate tax with a fair tax based on all new retail sales and services
Prohibits public institutions of higher learning from discriminating against religious, political, or ideological associations
Designates the "William Patrick Thompson Memorial Highway" in Webster County
Expresses support for actions taken to end motorcycle profiling
Proposes a constitutional amendment replacing individual and corporate income tax, and sales and use tax with a sales tax on retail sales of new tangible property and taxable services

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies standards for storage and use of gold and silver
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Reestablishes the Second Amendment Preservation Act
